name: app-mismatch
description: The GguiSession exists but is bound to a DIFFERENT app than the subscriber claims: `create-session` binds appId 'conformance-other', while the kit's runner always subscribes with appId 'conformance'. SPEC §12.2's subscribe-payload table makes the tenancy check a MUST — appId 'MUST match the GguiSession's bound appId or subscribe fails APP_MISMATCH' — and §12.2.3 keeps the code distinct from SESSION_NOT_FOUND (the GguiSession exists; it is reachable only from a different app), so clients route 'fix your appId / API key' differently from 're-handshake'. The runner-owned subscribe frame IS the probe (the inputEnvelope is descriptive, not dispatched); expected: an `error` frame with `payload.code === 'APP_MISMATCH'`.
